title: Root
aliases: Root: A Game of Woodland Might and Right

subtopics.title: What's in the Box
subtopics.content:
#### Player-Count Audit (from provided Law text)
- **2-player setup rule** — Remove all 4 Dominance cards before shuffling; each player still draws 3 cards.
- **Faction viability by count** — Viable faction mixes vary by player count (explicitly referenced to learning guide).
- **4+ player Vagabond rule** — Vagabond coalition option with Dominance card appears only in games with 4 or more players.

#### Explicit Shared Counts in the Provided Excerpt
- **Ambush cards** — 5 total (1 mouse, 1 rabbit, 1 fox, 2 bird).
- **Dominance cards** — 4 total (one per suit).
- **Ruins placed at setup** — 4 (one in each R slot).
- **Item supply pieces** — 12 total (2 M, 2 B, 1 C, 1 H, 2 S, 2 T, 2 X).
- **Faction overview cards** — 16.
- **Custom dice** — 2.

#### Faction Piece Counts Explicitly Shown
- **Marquise warriors in supply** — 25.
- **Marquise wood tokens in supply** — 8.
- **Marquise remaining buildings on tracks after starting placements** — 5 sawmills, 5 workshops, 5 recruiters.

#### Component Note
- **Faction piece limits** — All factions use only the pieces listed on the back of their faction boards.

subtopics.title: Board Layout
subtopics.content:
#### Standard Setup Sequence
1. **Assign factions and seating** — Randomly determine faction assignment, starting player, and seating order.
2. **Score markers** — Each player places marker on 0.
3. **Starting hands** — Shuffle deck and each player draws 3 cards.
4. **Ruins** — Place ruins in all 4 R slots.
5. **Item supply** — Place 2 M, 2 B, 1 C, 1 H, 2 S, 2 T, 2 X in matching item spaces.
6. **Shared aids** — Place 2 custom dice and distribute 16 faction overview cards as desired.
7. **Faction setup order** — In setup order (A, B, C, etc.), each player completes faction-specific setup from faction board/rules section.

#### Player-Count Setup Differences
--- 2 Players ---
- **Dominance card removal** — Remove all **4** Dominance cards from the shared deck before shuffling and drawing starting hands.
- **Faction mix guidance** — Viable faction combinations vary by player count; consult learning-to-play guide page 22 for recommended setups.

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content:
#### All Players
- **Faction board + pieces** — Take chosen faction board and all listed faction pieces.
- **Score marker** — Start at 0.
- **Hand** — Draw 3 cards from shared deck (after any 2-player Dominance removal).

#### Faction-Specific Setup (example explicitly shown in excerpt)
- **Marquise supply** — Gather 25 warriors and 8 wood tokens.
- **Marquise Keep** — Place Keep token in a corner clearing of choice.
- **Marquise garrison** — Place 1 warrior in each clearing except the diagonally opposite corner from the Keep.
- **Marquise starting buildings** — Place 1 sawmill, 1 workshop, 1 recruiter among Keep clearing and adjacent clearings.
- **Marquise track fill** — Put remaining buildings on tracks (5 sawmills, 5 workshops, 5 recruiters).

subtopics.title: Starting the Game
subtopics.content:
#### Before Turn 1
- **Golden-rule check** — Card text overrides rules reference in conflicts.
- **Absolute cannot rule** — Any rule saying cannot is absolute unless explicitly overridden.
- **Simultaneous effects** — Current-turn player chooses order unless noted otherwise.

#### First Turn Start
- **Turn order** — Play proceeds clockwise from starting player.
- **Phase order** — Birdsong → Daylight → Evening.s

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
#### Turn Structure (every player turn)
1. **Birdsong** — Resolve faction Birdsong effects in listed order.
2. **Daylight** — Take faction actions and common actions (Craft, Move, Battle) as allowed.
3. **Evening** — Resolve faction Evening effects, draw/discard requirements, and faction-specific scoring windows.

#### Loop Rule
- **Turn handoff** — After Evening, next clockwise player begins Birdsong.
- **Game continuation** — Repeat until an immediate win condition is met.

subtopics.title: Actions & Options
subtopics.content:
#### Craft
- **Cost match** — Activate crafting pieces matching suit icons in card cost.
- **Suit source** — Crafting piece suit equals suit of its clearing.
- **Activation limit** — Each crafting piece can be activated once per turn.
- **Immediate craft** — Resolve effect, then discard card.
- **Item craft** — If card grants an item, take matching item from supply; if unavailable, card cannot be crafted.
- **Persistent craft** — Place in play area for ongoing effect.
- **Duplicate lock** — Cannot craft duplicate persistent effect already in your play area.

#### Move
- **Move group size** — Move any number of your warriors or your pawn.
- **Path requirement** — Move between adjacent clearings.
- **Rule requirement** — You must rule origin, destination, or both.
- **Repeat movement allowed** — Same piece can move multiple times if effects allow.

#### Battle (ordered)
1. **Declare battle** — Choose clearing with your warriors/pawn and choose defender there.
2. **Ambush window** — Defender may play matching Ambush; attacker may foil with matching Ambush.
3. **Roll dice** — Attacker uses higher die, defender uses lower die.
4. **Hit limits** — Rolled hits capped by each side's warriors in battle clearing.
5. **Extra hits** — Add from abilities/effects; extra hits are not capped by warrior count.
6. **Deal hits simultaneously** — Remove pieces; casualty owner chooses pieces, but must remove own warriors before own buildings/tokens.

#### Cards and Information
- **Draw source** — Shared deck; reshuffle shared discard when deck is empty.
- **Discard/spend destination** — Shared discard unless rule says otherwise.
- **Bird wildcard rule** — Bird cards are wild for many spend/take/give prompts.
- **Forced-suit exception** — Some prompts force bird cards to count as the prompted suit.
- **Reverse substitution rule** — If asked for a bird card specifically, non-bird cards cannot substitute.
- **Public/private info** — Hand size is public; hand contents are private unless explicitly revealed.
- **Negotiation constraint** — Agreements are non-binding; card transfers only when explicitly allowed.

subtopics.title: Special Mechanics
subtopics.content:
#### Asymmetry Core
- **Faction engines differ** — Scoring systems, action economies, and risks differ by faction.
- **Shared framework** — Craft/Move/Battle/rule system is common, but faction text modifies heavily.

#### Rule Hierarchy
- **Card precedence** — Card text overrides rules reference if conflicting.
- **Reference precedence** — Rules reference overrides learning guide if conflicting.
- **Cannot clause** — Any rule using cannot is absolute unless explicitly overridden.

#### Ruling and Piece Typing
- **Rule formula** — Most warriors + buildings in a clearing rules it.
- **Tie state** — Ties mean no ruler.
- **Non-rule pieces** — Tokens and pawns do not count for rule.
- **Piece limits** — If you cannot place/remove full amount requested, do maximum possible.

#### Dominance System
- **Activation threshold** — During Daylight at 10+ VP, a player may activate a Dominance card.
- **Score-track exit** — Activated player removes score marker and no longer scores VP.
- **Dominance checks**
  - **Mouse/Rabbit/Fox Dominance** — Rule 3 clearings of matching suit at start of Birdsong.
  - **Bird Dominance** — Rule 2 opposite-corner clearings at start of Birdsong.
- **Persistence** — Activated Dominance card cannot be removed or swapped.

--- 2 Players ---
- **Coalition unavailable** — The Vagabond cannot form coalitions in 2-player games. Dominance cards are removed from the deck entirely.

--- 3 Players ---
- **Coalition unavailable** — The Vagabond cannot form coalitions in 3-player games. Dominance cards function normally for all factions.

--- 4 Players ---
- **Vagabond coalition** — The Vagabond may activate a Dominance card to form a coalition with the player with the fewest victory points (other than himself). If tied for fewest, the Vagabond chooses. If that player wins, the Vagabond also wins.

subtopics.title: Endgame & Scoring
subtopics.content:
#### Primary Victory
- **VP finish line** — First player to reach 30 VP wins immediately.
- **Simultaneous 30+ rule** — If multiple players reach 30+ simultaneously, current-turn player wins.

#### Shared VP Sources
- **Cardboard removal** — +1 VP for each enemy building or token removed.
- **Crafted item cards** — Score printed VP on crafted item card.
- **Faction scoring** — Additional VP from faction-specific systems in play.

#### Dominance Victory
- **Activation requirement** — Activate during Daylight at 10+ VP, then stop scoring VP.
- **Mouse/Rabbit/Fox condition** — Rule 3 matching-suit clearings at start of your Birdsong.
- **Bird condition** — Rule 2 opposite-corner clearings at start of your Birdsong.

#### Player-Count Endgame Differences

subtopics.title: Opening Priorities
subtopics.content:
#### Early Priorities for Any Faction
- **Read your faction board first** — Build opening around your faction's scoring engine, not generic moves.
- **Secure legal lanes** — Preserve clearings and rule states that keep your future Move/Battle options legal.
- **Pressure enemy cardboard** — Prioritize removing scoring infrastructure over pure warrior attrition when possible.
- **Protect suit flexibility** — Keep hand suits that your faction needs for near-term commitments.
- **Pick purposeful battles** — Fight when it scores, disrupts enemy tempo, or opens your own pathing.

#### Player-Count Opening Emphasis

subtopics.title: Common Mistakes
subtopics.content:
#### Common Heavy-Game Errors
- **Playing symmetrically** — Ignoring faction asymmetry and using one-size-fits-all plans.
- **Illegal move planning** — Forgetting you must rule origin or destination to move.
- **Bird-card misuse** — Spending birds recklessly and getting trapped by later suit requirements.
- **Warrior tunnel vision** — Removing warriors while leaving key enemy buildings/tokens untouched.
- **Trusting table deals as binding** — Agreements are non-binding by rule.
- **Bad Dominance timing** — Activating Dominance without realistic hold potential at Birdsong checks.
- **Battle-order mistakes** — Misplaying ambush/foil/timing and casualty order.

#### Player-Count Mistake Traps

subtopics.title: Key Decisions
subtopics.content:
#### Decision 1: VP Race vs Dominance Pivot
- **Stay on VP track** — Keep flexible scoring toward 30 VP.
- **Pivot to Dominance** — Trade VP scoring for map-control win condition at 10+ VP threshold.

#### Decision 2: Self-Tempo vs Leader Policing
- **Self-tempo line** — Spend actions on your own scoring engine growth.
- **Policing line** — Spend actions to slow the current leader's scoring trajectory.

#### Decision 3: Spend Cards Now vs Hold Suit Coverage
- **Spend now** — Convert cards into immediate craft/battle/action gains.
- **Hold coverage** — Preserve suits needed for next-turn obligations and defenses.

#### Player-Count Decision Context

metadata.notes: Written from provided Law of Root excerpt; exact counts included where explicitly present in excerpt, with faction-board-listed quantities referenced per rules text.
